title: MATH 102 - Algebra and Elementary Functions
description: In-depth treatment of exponential, logarithmic, trigonometric, and polynomial functions.

title: MATH 101 - College Algebra
description: Topics include algebraic equations and inequalities; polynomial, rational, algebraic, exponential, and logarithmic functions; compositions and inverses; geometric transformations and properties of functions; difference quotients.

title: MATH 101T - Trigonometry
description: Trigonometric functions, their graphs, inverses and applications, radian measure, solving triangles, trigonometric identities and equations, laws of sines and cosines, polar coordinates, vectors.

title: MATH 109B - Calculus 1 Part 1
description: Limits, continuity, derivatives, rates of change. First half of a two-semester sequence covering the same material covered in MATH 109 (Calculus 1) with just-in-time instruction for necessary algebra and trigonometry skills. Recommended for STEM students in Math placement category III or IV.

title: CS 111 - Computer Science Foundations 1
description: Introductory programming covering problem decomposition, control structures, simple data structures, testing, and documentation. Students design and implement a number of programs.

title: CS 112 - Computer Science Foundations 2
description: Object-oriented programming, focusing on classes, instances, methods, encapsulation, inheritance, overloading, multiple inheritance, and exception handling.

title: CS 201 - Requirements Engineering
description: Discuss methods for clearly specifying and managing software requirements. Utilize formal requirement syntax, semantics, and theory. Complete team project(s). Produce technical documents and make formal presentations.

title: CS 211 - Data Structures
description: Introduction to classic data structures and algorithms. Performance comparisons, big-O notation, trade-offs, arrays, linked lists, recursion, sorting, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, and hash tables.

title: CS 243 - Architecture
description: Introduction to computer architecture including assembly language, computer arithmetic, performance measures, datapath, control, pipelining, and memory/storage design.

title: MATH 107 - Introduction to Linear Algebra
description: Euclidean spaces, matrices and matrix arithmetic, solving systems of equations, and eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Emphasis on applications and use of computation.

title: MATH 109 - Calculus I
description: Limits, continuity, derivatives, integrals, and their applications.

title: MATH 110 - Calculus II
description: Logarithmic and exponential functions, inverse trigonometric functions, techniques of integration, infinite sequences and series, conic sections, polar coordinates.

title: MATH 253 - Discrete Mathematics
description: Sets, functions, relations, algorithms, induction, recursion, combinatorics, graphs, trees, and propositional logic.

title: STAT 109 - Introductory Biostatistics
description: Descriptive statistics, probability, random variables, discrete and continuous distributions, confidence intervals, contingency tests, regression and correlation, tests of hypothesis, analysis of variance. Emphasis: methods and applications used in the biological and natural resource sciences.

title: CS 312 - Algorithms
description: Introduction to algorithmic thinking. Recurrences and solution techniques, fundamental algorithms including graph algorithms, algorithm design techniques, balanced trees, performance trade-offs.

title: CS 325 - Database Design
description: Introduction to database design and implementation. Relational model, entity-relationship model and diagrams, converting a model to a schema, elementary Structured Query Language (SQL), normalization.

title: CS 328 - Web-Apps Using Databases
description: Building applications atop databases. N-tiered architecture; database tier: stored procedures/functions; presentation tier: web GUIs; application tier: controlling web-to-database interactions.

title: CS 356 - Software Design Engineering
description: Learn and practice software development lifecycle: secure design, modeling, architecture, verification and validation. Complete team projects, produce technical documents, and make formal presentations.

title: CS 357 - Software Project Management
description: Learn principles and practices of project management, including resource management, risk management, project manager responsibilities, and team building. Complete team projects, produce technical documents, and make formal presentations.

title: CS 374 - Operating Systems
description: Introduction to operating systems with an emphasis on process synchronization and control. Synchronization, kernel structure, scheduling, deadlock, virtual and physical memory, file and I/O.

title: CS 375 - Systems Programing
description: Develop and debug complex programs using debuggers, profilers, source version control systems, and integrated development environments. Analyze execution on modern computers. Measure and optimize performance. Complete team projects, produce technical documents, and make formal presentations.

title: CS 453 - Software Quality Assurance
description: Learn to assess and assure software quality with respect to client needs and software requirements specifications. Hands-on experience through field activities including client consultation. Complete team projects, produce technical documents, and make formal presentations.

title: CS 449 - Computer Security
description: Introduction to central concepts of computer security on networked systems. Topics include threats, cryptography, authentication, operating systems in security, legal and privacy issues.

title: CS 459 - Software Engineering Senior Project
description: Apply techniques from previous courses while working with actual clients to develop robust, ethical, and effective software.  Provide solutions for environmental and sustainability issues as well as cultural and socio-economic problems. Class is enhanced by seminars, tutorials, field activities, and client consultation, and culminates in a significant team project.

title: CS 232 - Python Programming
description: Introduction to the Python language. Idiomatic language features such as lists, dictionaries, tuples, and sets. Use of Python classes and modules to accomplish complex tasks.

title: CS 235 - Java Programming
description: Object-oriented programming; event handling; abstract windowing toolkit applets, applications; Java database connectivity; applications programming interface and Java doc.

title: CS 279 - Introduction to Linux
description: Introduces the UNIX/Linux family of operating systems. Basic commands, utilities, system structures, scripting and tools are explored. Elements of system administration are presented.

title: CS 346 - Telecommunications and Networks
description: Introduction to the fundamentals of telecommunication and to the structure, implementation, and theoretical underpinnings of computer networking.

title: CS 444 - Robotics
description: A project-based introduction to robotic systems and software that controls them, including gearing, mechanics, Al control systems, and problem solving with robots.

title: CS 461 - Computational Models
description: An introduction to the Chomsky hierarchy, automata, Church- Turing Thesis, computability, NP-completeness, and information theory.

title: CS 480 - Advanced Topics in Computing
description: Advanced topics in computer science.
